name: Xinzo - Rio Salas Loop
description: This moderate 40.1-mile road cycling route offers scenic views of the Rio Salas Bridge and reservoir, requiring good fitness.
region_intro: Road cycling around Xinzo De Limia offers diverse terrain across the A Limia region, characterized by expansive, partially flooded flat fields flanked by round-top mountains. The area features mostly well-paved routes, with some segments on more challenging rides potentially unpaved. Cyclists can explore landscapes ranging from wooded groves along riverbanks to the rich flora of the Baixa Limia-Serra do Xurés Natural Park, which includes deciduous forests and Mediterranean elements. The region's geology is abundant in granite materials, contributing to its distinct natural features. Best road cycling routes around Xinzo De Limia The most popular road cycling route is Xinzo - Celanova Loop, a 44.6 miles (71.7 km) trail that takes 2 hours 47 minutes to complete. This difficult route features over 3,000 feet (1036 meters) of elevation gain. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Xinzo - Rio Salas Loop, a moderate 40.1 miles (64.5 km) path. This route offers picturesque views of the Rio Salas Bridge and reservoir. Local road cyclists also love the Junta da Tona Cow Sculpture – Alto do Furriolo climb loop from Xinzo de Limia, a 37.9 miles (61.0 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 2 hours 56 minutes. Road cycling around Xinzo De Limia is defined by expansive flat fields, round-top mountains, and wooded groves. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to challenging climbs. The routes in Xinzo De Limia are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from 5 reviews. More than 300 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Xinzo De Limia's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Xinzo De Limia?
A: There are over 45 road cycling routes around Xinzo De Limia, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when road cycling in Xinzo De Limia?
A: The region of A Limia, where Xinzo De Limia is located, features a distinctive landscape with expansive, partially flooded flat fields flanked by round-top mountains. You'll find mostly well-paved surfaces, though some segments on more challenging rides might be unpaved. The routes also pass through wooded groves along riverbanks and oak groves bordering cultivated lands, with granite materials abundant throughout the Limia basin.

Q: Are there routes suitable for beginners or those looking for an easier ride?
A: Yes, Xinzo De Limia offers routes suitable for various abilities. While many routes are moderate or difficult, there are at least 2 easy road cycling routes available. For a moderate option with less elevation, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31477228" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;A Sainza Crosses – Ponte Linares loop from Xinzo de Limia&lt;/a&gt;, which has a relatively low elevation gain.

Q: What about more challenging road cycling routes in the area?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, Xinzo De Limia has over 20 difficult routes. A notable challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e936541546/xinzo-celanova-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Xinzo - Celanova Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a 44.6-mile route featuring over 3,000 feet (1036 meters) of elevation gain.

Q: When is the best time of year to go road cycling in Xinzo De Limia?
A: The region is generally pleasant for cycling from mid-April to late June, which is also an excellent time for birdwatching. Summers can be warm, so early mornings or late afternoons are often preferred. Spring and autumn offer milder temperatures and beautiful scenery.

Q: What natural features or viewpoints can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: You can enjoy picturesque views of the Rio Salas Bridge and reservoir, as seen on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e936997496/xinzo-rio-salas-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Xinzo - Rio Salas Loop&lt;/a&gt;. The region's unique landscape, shaped by the historic Antela lagoon, offers expansive flat fields and round-top mountains. The Baixa Limia-Serra do Xurés Natural Park, part of a cross-border Biosphere Reserve, provides diverse flora and fauna, including deciduous and Mediterranean forests.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or cultural sites to explore while cycling?
A: Yes, the area is rich in history. You can find remnants of Roman influence, such as sections of the Vía Nova and the Roman camp of Aquis Querquennis. Medieval defense towers like A Forxa, Sandías, Pena, and Celme stand as historical landmarks. The Mosteiro do Bon Xesús de Trandeiras is another significant cultural symbol in the comarca. For specific points of interest, consider visiting the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6309837" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pilgrims' Hostel with Scallop Shell Messages&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5291176" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fonte do Santo&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any scenic loop routes for road cycling?
A: Yes, many routes in Xinzo De Limia are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Popular scenic loop options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e936997496/xinzo-rio-salas-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Xinzo - Rio Salas Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1272057" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Maceda – Acea River Beach loop from Xinzo de Limia&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Is parking available for cyclists in Xinzo De Limia?
A: Xinzo De Limia, as a key town in the area, generally offers various parking options. Specific parking availability for trailheads can vary, but you'll typically find public parking within the town or near popular starting points for routes.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in Xinzo De Limia?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars from 5 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse terrain, ranging from expansive flat fields to challenging climbs, and the scenic views of the natural landscape, including the Rio Salas reservoir and the unique A Limia region.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling routes available from Xinzo De Limia?
A: While many routes are loops around the immediate area, the region also serves as a starting point for longer endurance rides. For example, routes extending towards Pontevedra (99.9 km) or Santiago de Compostela (146.1 km) are available for those looking for multi-day or extensive cycling adventures.

Q: Can I find family-friendly road cycling options in Xinzo De Limia?
A: While many routes involve moderate to difficult terrain, families with older children or those comfortable with longer distances might find some of the easier or flatter sections suitable. It's recommended to review the elevation profiles and distances of routes to ensure they match your family's fitness level.
tips: Bridge between Spain and Portugal. Amazing view and to take photo. There’s a small village ( south) beautiful but narrow roads

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Xinzo - Rio Salas Vuelta?
faqs.answer: The route is conveniently located with a parking lot available at its starting point in Xinzo de Limia. This makes it easy to begin your ride without hassle.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: The Xinzo - Rio Salas Vuelta primarily features well-paved surfaces, with about 57.4 km of asphalt and 6.56 km of paved roads. While mostly smooth, be aware of very small segments of unknown surface (353 m) and cobblestones (241 m). There might also be some unpaved sections that could be challenging for road bikes, so be prepared for varied conditions.

faqs.question: What scenic highlights can I expect along the route?
faqs.answer: A major highlight is the beautiful Rio Salas Bridge, which offers stunning views as it crosses a reservoir fed by the Salas River. You'll also enjoy the surrounding mountainous landscape and the picturesque reservoir itself, especially near the border between Spain and Portugal. There's also a small, charming village south of the bridge.

faqs.question: Is the Xinzo - Rio Salas Vuelta suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Komoot rates this route as 'moderate', indicating it requires good fitness. While much of it is well-paved and enjoyable, the 720 meters of elevation gain over 64.5 kilometers, combined with potential unpaved sections, means it's not ideal for absolute beginners. Cyclists with some experience and good stamina will find it more rewarding.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle the Xinzo - Rio Salas Vuelta?
faqs.answer: Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant cycling conditions with milder temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summers can be hot, and winters might bring colder, wetter weather. Always check the local forecast before you head out.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required to cycle this route, especially within Parque Natural Baixa Limia-Serra do Xurés?
faqs.answer: No specific permits or entrance fees are generally required to cycle this route or access the public areas of Parque Natural Baixa Limia-Serra do Xurés. However, always respect local regulations, especially regarding protected natural areas, and stay on marked paths.

faqs.question: Is the Xinzo - Rio Salas Vuelta dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While road cycling with dogs can be challenging due to traffic and speed, if your dog is accustomed to running alongside a bike and you can ensure their safety, it might be possible. However, given it's a road cycling route, it's generally not recommended for dogs unless they are in a trailer or very well-trained and safe on roads. Always keep them leashed if you stop in populated areas or near wildlife.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route briefly intersects with several other paths. You'll find short segments that overlap with trails such as O Camiño Privilexiado, Camiño da Revolta, Camiño da Lama, and Camiño da Ponte Vella, among others. These intersections are typically very brief and do not significantly alter the primary road cycling experience.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or restaurants along the Xinzo - Rio Salas Vuelta?
faqs.answer: The route passes through some villages, and while specific establishments aren't guaranteed to be open, you'll likely find options for refreshments in Xinzo de Limia at the start/end. It's always a good idea to carry enough water and snacks, especially for the longer stretches between towns.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a road bike ride on this route?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate difficulty and length, you should bring your road bike in good working order, a helmet, appropriate cycling attire, plenty of water, snacks, a basic repair kit (spare tube, pump, tire levers), and a fully charged phone. Sunscreen and sunglasses are also recommended, especially during sunnier months.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Xinzo - Rio Salas Vuelta?
faqs.answer: On average, cyclists can expect to complete this 64.5-kilometer route in approximately 3 hours and 6 minutes. This duration can vary based on your fitness level, the number of stops you make for photos, and weather conditions.
